translation: 2nd December, 8:47 a.m. Supreme Court of Judicature, Defendant's Antechamber 3
That morning, I was seething with rage.
That my dearest friend should be made to suffer not once, but twice...
...Naruhodo!
Ah! Asogi...! You made it in time!
Yes. I've only just arrived, after a steamship and an overnight train.
Uuugh... Thank goodness. I was afraid you wouldn't make it.
I... I simply don't know what to do any more.
Don't lose heart, Naruhodo. When I say I'll come, you can be sure I will.
(All the more so...when a friend is in need.)
...Asogi...
This is my friend, Ryunosuke Naruhodo. We are classmates at Yumei University.
Ten days ago, he stood in the dock at the Supreme Court of Judicature, accused of murder.
Though only a student, he proved his innocence by his own hand.
A remarkable feat: acting as his own defence counsel while also the accused...
No, no, Asogi. I can't take the credit for that.
I only managed it thanks to the brilliant student lawyer standing beside me.
Nonsense. Your defence was magnificent. There is no other word for it.
(Standing beside you that day, I saw it plainly.)
(Naruhodo...you have the makings of a lawyer.)
Well...as far as I'm concerned...
I certainly never expected to set foot in a courtroom again.
...Kazuma-sama. Thank you for coming such a very long way.
Ah, Miss Susato. Thank you for the telegram.
‘ANOTHER SUMMONS ARRIVED. RETURN AT ONCE. TRIAL IN FIVE DAYS, 9 A.M. NARUHODO’
I spent two minutes puzzling over what the final ‘Naruhodo’ was supposed to mean...
I... I am terribly sorry.
I sent Mr Naruhodo's telegram exactly as he had written it, you see...
But we're friends! You should have recognised my name at once!
But is your name not ‘Naruhodou’, with a U at the end?
There wasn't room for the U.
A telegram costs two sen more once it exceeds thirty characters.
So you saved two sen...and cost me two minutes.
...As close as ever, I see.
This is Susato Mikotoba. An exceptionally capable judicial assistant.
She always assists me in my work as a lawyer.
She's a dependable ally whom I trust with all my heart.
...That is the highest praise I could wish for.
...It was the day after the trial of my friend, Ryunosuke Naruhodo.
...I had taken a steamship from Yokohama to Shikoku.
Before finally departing for Great Britain...I meant to pay my respects at the Asogi family home.
But as soon as the telegram reached me, I leapt aboard the next overnight train.
...So that I might save my friend with my own two hands...
Um...Kazuma-sama?
If you only arrived this morning...what does the summons say?
Strictly speaking, I did not arrive ‘this morning’. I arrived just now.
I stopped at home to collect it, but...I haven't even broken the seal yet.
W-What?! Then you mean...
Yes. I know next to nothing about this latest charge.
However...I did hear about your recent trial...
Apparently the prosecutor objected and brought a fresh charge three days after the verdict.
Y-Yes! I thought there must have been some mistake...
Say no more, Naruhodo. ...I understand everything.
Huh?
I hear Prosecutor Auchi's reputation suffered a serious blow in that trial.
This new charge is most likely...his idea of revenge.
R-Revenge...?
He means to cause us trouble with a baseless accusation.
...What an awful nuisance...
Still...I can almost see how the prosecutor feels.
...What did you say?
He was bested by a mere university student, at the Supreme Court, before all those distinguished people.
And on top of that...
This is exactly why our country's justice system cannot be trusted!
A-Asogi...
To use the Supreme Court to harass a student... What an outrageous abuse of authority!
He's nothing but an outlaw, brandishing violence in the name of the law!
Indeed. The Prosecutors Office has lately been criticised in the papers for its heavy-handed methods.
My own prosecution last time was rather heavy-handed, as a matter of fact...
Honestly, I've found it difficult to trust the police ever since.
And yet...sadly...
The police and prosecutors are not the only ones who lack the public's trust.
We lawyers are viewed much the same way.
R-Really...?
Evidently you haven't been reading the papers, Naruhodo.
N-No...I haven't. I've had rather more pressing things to worry about lately.
I purchased this morning's paper. ...Is this the article in question?
‘PROMINENT LAWYER ARRESTED!’ ...It's the front-page headline.
Ah, as expected of Miss Susato. Ever prepared.
Apparently a lawyer tried to bribe an officer of the judiciary.
So this one is ‘bribery’, is it? Yes, that's appalling.
The legal profession was established in our country only recently.
Dismissed as mere pettifogging shysters, our standing remains very low.
At a time when we need to earn the public's trust, this scandal is particularly damaging.
...Naruhodo. You ought at least to read about matters such as these.
Uuugh... I've never been very good with newspapers...
Read them whether you like them or not. You're a university student here in the capital, aren't you?
What is it?
You...have a habit of seeing straight through my ‘inner voice’.
...Because your ‘inner voice’ has a habit of becoming an audible monologue.
The newspaper article has been added to the Court Record.
...In any case...
I don't know what that prosecutor hopes to accomplish at this late stage...
...but today, I will stand at the defence bench. Is that clear, Naruhodo?
What?! B-But that's...
No, say no more, Naruhodo. ...I understand everything.
Ten days ago, you deliberately refused to let me defend you in court.
I understand the thoughtfulness behind that decision. However...
I should at least be allowed to clear up the aftermath myself.
B-But, Kazuma-sama...
Say no more, Miss Susato. That prosecutor is no match for me.
I will save you, without fail. ...Leave everything to me.
Um...Kazuma-sama? There is just one thing I should confirm...
...Defendant!
The Supreme Court is about to convene! Proceed to the courtroom at once!
...It appears the time has come.
Miss Susato...I'm counting on you.
........... Of course.
Don't look so glum, Naruhodo. We shall meet again shortly...my friend.
...In the courtroom!
........... Y-Yes. All right.
...My name is Kazuma Asogi.
I am a second-year student at Yumei University...and a qualified lawyer who stands in court.
And now...this month, my dearest wish will be fulfilled. I will cross the sea to Great Britain.
I will go as a visiting student...to study the latest developments in British law.
This will most likely be my final courtroom appearance in Japan.
...And this time, I will save my friend from peril with my own two hands!
The same day, 9:00 a.m. Supreme Court of Judicature, Courtroom 1
The court will now hear the case before us.
...The prosecution is ready, Your Excellency.
...The defence is ready, Your Excellency.
...‘Ready’, you say...?
It would seem the student is ignorant of one of the Supreme Court's cardinal rules.
What are you talking about?
The Supreme Court admits no women! I'm talking to you, standing there as cool as you please!
Ah...
Feigning ignorance won't help you! Leave this courtroom at once!
...With the greatest respect...
I understand today's hearing concerns a personal complaint brought by Prosecutor Auchi.
W-Well, what of it?!
For such a matter to be heard by the Supreme Court is itself highly irregular.
...This hearing has been permitted as a special exception for today.
Then, while we are making special exceptions...
...I ask that a female judicial assistant be permitted for today as well.
Absurd! You cannot possibly—
...Very well. I shall make a special allowance.
WHAAAAAAT?!
(...Miss Susato is formidable.)
This is, after all, a special court. ...I suggest you turn a blind eye to minor irregularities.
Grrrrrr...
Now then. More importantly...Kazuma Asogi.
Yes, Your Excellency.
Surely...you do not intend to act as defence counsel today?
...I do.
I intend to demolish every baseless allegation the prosecution may raise.
Hmm...
Heh heh heh... ‘Baseless allegations’, you say...?
...Prosecutor Auchi.
You appear not to understand the position you are in.
Naruhodo's trial ten days ago was settled conclusively.
The true culprit confessed. The case is already over.
...You of all people should have learned that beyond any doubt, Prosecutor Auchi!
...It would seem...
...that this hearing, too, has reached its conclusion.
What say you, Prosecutor Auchi?
I-Impossible! I, Taketsuchi Auchi...
...defeated by a rank amateur of a university student?!
Ah, yes. I seem to recall that little farce.
However...it would seem...
...that you are the one who fails to understand his position, my dear student.
Wh-What?!
...To begin with...
...is a man who has not even read the summons qualified to stand at the defence bench?
.........! (H-How does he know?)
Um...Kazuma-sama. Forgive my impertinence, but...
...I strongly recommend that you examine the summons now.
I added it to the Court Record a short while ago.
The Court Record...?
(Touch the Court Record button to examine the evidence in detail.)
(...Perhaps I should take this opportunity to look through it.)
Now then...read the summons carefully, and let us hear your answer, student.
Let us begin with the obvious. State the name of the defendant in today's trial!
Th-That's obvious! The question scarcely needs asking!
The person accused in this trial... The defendant's name is...
...Ryunosuke Naruhodo, without question!
‘Without question’, you say...?
He may be a little scatterbrained and rather careless. However...
...he has harmed no one. That has already been proven!
...Heh heh heh heh...
If your friend is ‘a little scatterbrained’ and ‘rather careless’...
...then you are exceedingly scatterbrained and utterly careless!
Wh-What?!
The court is appalled by the defence's muddle-headed blundering.
NOOOOOO! (Wh-What does he mean?)
...Kazuma-sama! Please, just examine the summons!
Touch the Court Record button at once... I beg you!
(I have never seen Miss Susato look so desperate.)
(I should touch the Court Record button and examine the evidence.)
...The defence will now present its answer once again.
Erm...would that make the defendant ‘Susato Mikotoba’?
...Kazuma-sama. Thank you for everything you have done for me.
I shall board a train at once and set off on a long journey.
Ah...no! That is certainly not what I meant!
The exhaustion from running here from the station this morning must finally be catching up with me...
The defendant is the person who has been accused!
Kindly conduct your young lovers' quarrel in the antechamber.
(...I feel like cutting everything short and starting again from the antechamber.)
The defendant's name is written at the very top of the summons!
Naturally, the accused can only be Kazuma Asogi!
A-A...sogi Kazuma...?
(But my friend's name is...Ryunosuke Naruhodo, surely...)
That is correct. The person accused on this occasion is...
M-M-MEEEEEEEE?!
Order! Order in the court!
To be quite honest, I too was surprised when I read the summons...
The stated offence is assault. Is that correct?
You, student, would do well to cast your mind back.
Remember that scene from before...and what happened next!
Wh-What happened next...?
...It would seem...
...that this trial, too, has finally reached its conclusion.
What say you, Prosecutor Auchi?
I-Impossible! I, Taketsuchi Auchi...
...defeated by a rank amateur of a university student?!
Your future advancement as a prosecutor will be difficult from this day forward.
Grrrrrrrrr... Ryunosuke Naruhodo!
Y-Yes?
The Auchi clan will remember this grudge until the end of its line!
...The proud and arrogant are forever brought low by the young.
It is the inevitable course of history. ...See that you engrave this feeling upon your heart!
Though a hundred generations may pass, no descendant of yours shall ever best a Naruhodo!
...Whether that country bumpkin's verdict was correct, whether that case was solved...
...none of it matters! It has nothing whatsoever to do with the present complaint!
N-No... Surely not...
Only one fact matters here...
The topknot that symbolised Taketsuchi Auchi was lopped off with a single chop!
A-And that...constitutes the alleged assault?
...Precisely so.
H-How utterly...
The man still has the katana dangling from his waist, Your Excellency!
To slash at a man with a katana in the Supreme Court... What an outrageous act!
He brandishes violence itself! A literal outlaw!
Uuuuuuuugh!
In truth, the trial was already as good as over before that moment.
Now that the time has come, I, Auchi, shall confess...
My knees were knocking. I could barely remain standing.
And yet! Oh, and yet!
Poor Auchi even had his precious topknot chopped clean away!
Could any act be more excessive? No! I say it could not!
B-But...
I, Auchi, have a son about your age, you know.
And he does not go about brandishing a katana in his university classroom!
Hmm... Now that you put it that way...
...perhaps that final blow really was entirely unnecessary.
Ghh...!
And still you stand there today, your headband fluttering without a care!
Well? What do you have to say for yourself?!
(...Something about this doesn't sit right with me, but...)
(...if drawing the katana was excessive, I have no answer to that.)
I sincerely apologise. It will never happen again.
K-Kazuma-sama...
Now that the defendant has bowed his head in a frank and honourable apology...
...I believe we may consider the matter resolved.
...Heh heh heh... Not so fast, Your Excellency.
If a neat bow of the head settled every matter, there would be no need for a Supreme Court!
Wh-What...?
The crime of bringing a katana into court. The crime of attacking me with it.
And...one still greater crime besides.
The heinous crime of leaving my head forever unable to bear a topknot!
For all these offences, the prosecution demands a custodial sentence of six months!
Wh-What?! Six months...in prison...?
I cannot say I understand the final charge concerning his inability to wear a topknot...
...but if a lawyer drew a katana in the Supreme Court, ran amok and caused injury...
...six months' imprisonment would seem a plausible sentence!
No, no. I don't believe I ‘ran amok’...
(This is unthinkable...)
(Auchi... I never imagined he would demand an actual prison sentence.)
Heh heh heh...
Well, now. By bowing your head so frankly and honourably...
...are you declaring your acceptance of the prosecution's proposed sentence?
B-But...
Y-You mustn't, Kazuma-sama! If such a sentence were imposed...
...your imminent study tour to Great Britain would be cancelled!
It would appear the defence has no objection.
Your Excellency, I believe you may now deliver your verdict...
...I beg to differ, Prosecutor Auchi.
The defence cannot accept the prosecution's assertion!
M-Miss Mikotoba!
Oh? A mere judicial assistant dares to interject during proceedings...?
What is the meaning of this, judicial assistant?
Y-Yes! Well...that is...
Even if a katana were swung wildly about here in the courtroom...
S-Swung wildly...?
...however long the blade, it could never reach the prosecution bench!
In other words, Prosecutor Auchi's claim is nothing but a baseless allegation!
It seems...we are fated to do battle once again.
You mean to say the prosecution is prepared to proceed?
Prepared to prove Kazuma Asogi's alleged assault...?
...Naturally.
Perfect testimony...and perfect evidence.
The trial has only just begun, Your Excellency.
...Very well. The prosecution will proceed.
Bring forth the witnesses who will prove Kazuma Asogi's crime!
It would be my very great pleasure...
(At first, I thought this case utterly ridiculous...)
(But if I lose, my path to Great Britain will be closed to me...)
(...If this is a dream, let me wake from it at once!)
...Witnesses, state your names and occupations for the court.
...Yes, sir. My name is Satoru Hosonaga.
I am currently employed at the Hair Hunters barbershop, giving our patrons modern cropped styles.
Erm...my name is Ryunosuke Naruhodo. I'm a second-year student at Yumei University...
...and until about ten days ago, I was being held in solitary confinement for a crime I didn't commit.
Y-You! Why are you standing over there?!
Well, you see...
Mr Naruhodo is a witness...for the prosecution.
F-For the prosecution...?
Put plainly, he is your enemy, Kazuma-sama.
That's why the first thing I said when we met in the antechamber was...
...that I had no idea what I was supposed to do, Asogi.
Then why did you not tell me so at once?!
I tried to tell you over and over! But every time...
...you said, ‘Say no more,’ and silenced me!
...Mrrgh.
This country bumpkin was present at that fateful trial ten days ago...
...and witnessed the defendant's crime from closer than anyone else.
H-His ‘crime’...?
...As for me...
...I may look every inch a barber, but that is merely a disguise.
My true identity is...an undercover detective!
...Yes, I certainly remember you.
(Detective Hosonaga... He testified at my trial ten days ago.)
The witnesses will now give their testimony.
Tell the court of the defendant's despicable crime that day...and...
...the tragic consequences that followed!
...We shall tell it exactly as it happened.
— Asogi's ‘Crime’ —
It's true. He swung his katana right before my eyes.
B-But that doesn't necessarily mean it cut the prosecutor's head!
Mr Auchi is a regular patron of the Hair Hunters barbershop...
Ever since that day, the hair that was so sharply severed has apparently stopped growing altogether.
He can never wear a topknot again...a fatal wound for Mr Auchi, I'm told.
Good heavens! His hair has ceased to grow...?
That is a most grave affliction.
...How can you possibly declare that it has ‘stopped’ growing?!
It has only been ten days!
I, Auchi, know my own hair!
A green youngster whose head sprouts like a meadow could never understand...
...the death rattle of the last withered reed in a barren waste!
...The situation appears to be very serious indeed.
This is absurd...
The Meiji era cries out for ‘civilisation and enlightenment’, and the topknot is disappearing...
But I! I alone...!
...had sworn to spend my entire life together with my topknot!
You might as well take the opportunity to get a nice modern crop.
What was that, you would-be murderer?!
(Your ‘inner voice’ is escaping quite plainly, Naruhodo.)
...If the testimony we have just heard is entirely true...
...the defendant may require severe punishment.
(My study tour to Great Britain...cancelled?!)
...Kazuma Asogi. Conduct your cross-examination with the utmost care.
Y-Yes, Your Excellency!
— Asogi's ‘Crime’ —
It's true. He swung his katana right before my eyes.
B-But that doesn't necessarily mean it cut the prosecutor's head!
Mr Auchi is a regular patron of the Hair Hunters barbershop...
Ever since that day, the hair that was so sharply severed has apparently stopped growing altogether.
He can never wear a topknot again...a fatal wound for Mr Auchi, I'm told.
I could not forgive that topknot for needlessly driving you into a corner.
No, I was glad! It was rather cathartic, actually.
Yes, because you cathartically chopped my topknot clean off!
So if everyone found it cathartic...perhaps no harm was done...?
I think not!
I, Taketsuchi Auchi, shall never forsake the topknot, not even unto the last of my line!
...The Supreme Court strongly recommends that it be forsaken within your own generation.
Anyway, I was standing right beside him. There is something I must make clear!
He certainly flashed his blade...but that is all I saw!
What do you mean by that, Ryunosuke Naruhodo?
Think about it normally. There is no possible way the blade could have reached.
He swung his katana at the defence bench, yet supposedly cut a topknot at the prosecution bench.
Hmm... Indeed. That is quite true.
Although Asogi does get carried away after finishing a large beef hotpot...
...and every time, he makes the strangest boasts.
Boasts...?
He claims that with the Asogi family's secret sword-drawing art...
...he can split a watermelon seven metres away clean in two.
Obviously that's impossible. But he has a habit of saying such things...
Naruhodo...do you mock the Asogi family's art of sword-drawing?
Huh?
Bring me a watermelon at once! I shall cut a portion for everyone present!
Well...it is December. Watermelons are rather out of season.
Then bring a pumpkin! It is somewhat harder, but worth the attempt!
Did you hear that, Your Excellency?
The man has admitted it himself. The crime was possible!
K-Kazuma-sama... What have you done?!
M-My apologies.
When the Asogi sword-drawing art is called into question...I cannot help myself.
I am overcome by the urge to split a watermelon in two on the spot.
Heh heh heh... It appears the time has come...
...to present the decisive evidence.
My topknot unquestionably fell prey to a blade...
...and this evidence proves it beyond all doubt!
Wh-What...?
Behold...this!
This twisted paper cord...appears to have been cleanly severed.
Japanese paper cord is the traditional means of tying a topknot.
The important question is...how was the cord severed?
Ah! This is...
The cut across the cord is extraordinarily clean. Without question...
...it was sliced by a blade of the finest quality and sharpness!
What?! I-It was cut by a katana...?
What say you, counsel... No, defendant!
With my sword-drawing art...and the famed blade Karuma at my side...
...severing only the cord without touching a single hair would be child's play!
May the court interpret that last remark as a declaration of guilt?
It may not!
In any event, the paper cord will be admitted into evidence.
The severed paper cord has been added to the Court Record.
It is true. I severed this cord that day.
And precisely the cord alone. This magnificent cut proves it.
...Are you still boasting?
...It appears the defence has placed itself in considerable jeopardy.
In any case, the cross-examination will now continue.
...It appears my time has come.
Detective Hosonaga...are you undercover in a barbershop this time?
...Yes. I have been gathering information there for the past three years.
...Isn't that rather a long time?
A barbershop is a gentleman's social salon, where all manner of information changes hands.
Even the most tight-lipped gentleman cannot help revealing secrets to his barber.
My patrons particularly enjoy the soothing rhythm of my scissors.
I will permit no other man to touch my topknot!
There can be no higher praise...sir.
......... Are you truly a detective?
‘If a thing is worth doing, it is worth doing perfectly.’
...That is my creed.
Twice a week, Hosonaga the hunter of criminals becomes a Hair Hunter.
(...Detective Hosonaga is beginning to seem rather impressive.)
...I would say he is extraordinary rather than impressive.
C-Can such a thing really happen...?
Hair is an exceedingly delicate thing.
After a terrifying ordeal, it may even shrink back in fear.
That is why the secret is to cut it with the utmost tenderness and care.
...I find myself wanting to visit the Hair Hunters barbershop.
...As do I.
And yet you!
You mercilessly slaughtered my darling hairs and scattered them to the wind!
They were in the prime of their growth! Hairs with their whole futures ahead of them!
...Kazuma-sama is beginning to look like the villain here.
Mrrrgh... (Something about this seems very wrong...)
N-Never again...?
Well...that is according to Mr Auchi.
His lustrous black hair was sliced through, and thereafter ceased growing.
Naturally, he can expect no more than what he has now...
But...has it truly stopped growing?!
......... I could not say.
After all, only ten days have passed since that trial.
No one knows Auchi's hair better than Auchi himself!
...On that day, I heard it distinctly.
The death screams of a million hairs...atop my very head!
...That figure may be somewhat exaggerated.
(...That concludes the testimony.)
What do you think, Kazuma-sama?
I will not surrender my study tour to Britain over a topknot!
That cry from the depths of your soul...is entirely understandable.
Then you must make your case forcefully to His Excellency.
The blade could never have reached the prosecutor from the defence bench!
First, I should press every statement carefully and gather information.
...An excellent approach!
The witness's testimony clearly contradicts this evidence!
...Regrettably...
...that evidence does not appear to have an edge quite as sharp as your glare.
(...Do my eyes truly look that savage?)
I object to the witness's last statement!
An objection made with your headband floundering so lacks conviction.
My eyes may wander, but please overlook the headband...
(...It appears I should review the testimony once more.)
I apologise for cutting the cord that bound the topknot.
...However...
...I cannot allow the prosecution's malicious lie to stand.
Wh-Wh-What?!
This cord was severed in a perfectly straight line.
In other words, the blade touched nothing whatsoever except the cord.
Nothing...except the cord...?
Aaaaaaaaaah!
Wh-What is the matter, witness?!
Come to think of it...I remember that moment with perfect clarity!
...The proud and arrogant are forever brought low by the young.
It is the inevitable course of history. ...See that you engrave this feeling upon your heart!
...The topknot was certainly cut cleanly. But...
...in that instant, not a single strand of hair was scattered!
...Precisely. What I cut that day was...
...not his hair, but the paper cord!
S-So...what of it?!
It changes nothing about the fact that you brandished a katana in court!
...Prosecutor Auchi. You made the following assertion.
‘The defendant left Prosecutor Auchi unable ever to wear a topknot again.’
Mr Hosonaga testified that this was the fatal wound.
But that is impossible. And the reason is obvious.
I did not cut even a single millimetre of hair. Therefore...
...even now, you remain perfectly able to tie your beloved topknot!
Wh...
WHAAAAAAAAAT?!
Bailiff! Bring me a length of Japanese paper cord at once!
It is as though we have returned to ten days ago.
...I believe the truth is now perfectly clear.
The prosecution's charge was, as suspected, nothing more than a baseless allegation!
...And in light of this...
...the claim that his hair has ‘stopped growing’ must be regarded with considerable suspicion as well.
In another few months, the truth will reveal itself naturally.
Although...by that time, I will already be in Great Britain.
...Mrrrrrgh...
Indeed. Since the prosecution's claim contained a falsehood...
...the complaint is dismissed. These proceedings are hereby concluded.
GRAAAAAAAAAH! CURSE YOUUUUUUUU!
Congratulations, Kazuma-sama!
Now you can depart for Great Britain with your name cleared!
Yes. For a time, I wondered what would become of me...
The study tour to Great Britain has been my dream for many years.
...And now, at last, that dream appears certain to come true.
...This concludes today's proceedings.
...With the court's permission, I, Taketsuchi Auchi...
...object to that man's study tour!
Wh-What are you saying?! You object to my study tour?!
What is the meaning of this, Prosecutor Auchi?
A visiting judicial student is an envoy who serves, in a sense, as the face of our nation.
The candidates considered were not limited to students of law.
Distinguished serving judges, prosecutors and lawyers were also nominated.
The Ministry of Justice then conducted a rigorous final selection.
And the man selected was Kazuma Asogi.
...However...
...there was a certain impropriety behind his selection.
Wh-What?!
We have obtained proof of that impropriety. Is that not so, witness?!
Of course not. I am plainly addressing the man beside you.
Yes... It is true that I conducted an investigation.
(Detective Hosonaga...?)
The visiting students are due to set sail at the end of this very month...
...and I have heard nothing of any impropriety in their selection.
If this claim is true, it is a matter of the utmost gravity.
It appears we must hear the witness's testimony once more.
A most wise decision, Your Excellency.
Detective Hosonaga, report the findings of your investigation!
...Yes, sir. Very well.
— Impropriety in the Study Tour Selection —
The Ministry of Justice conducted the selection process over a number of stages.
There were written and practical exams, negotiation tests...and finally each candidate was awarded a score.
I was ordered to go undercover and obtained the confidential records.
According to those records, Kazuma Asogi did not place first. He was the runner-up.
...It is Prosecutor Auchi's belief that this indicates an impropriety in the selection.
Th-The runner-up...?
I was not...the first-choice candidate...?
Hah! Do not flatter yourself, you freshly sprouted stripling in a headband!
...Ghh!
‘A student lawyer goes to Great Britain.’ It makes an appealing story for the public.
I imagine the Japanese government was guided by some such unscrupulous calculation.
U... UWAAAAAAAAAAH!
S-Stay with me, Asogi! The wound is not deep!
To me...yes! You will always be the first-choice candidate!
...Please stop, Naruhodo. You are only compounding my misery.
......... Kazuma Asogi.
Y-Yes, Your Excellency!
The selection process was rigorous. ...Of that, I can personally assure you.
If you are the rightful visiting judicial student...then prove it yourself.
(In other words...he is asking me to demonstrate my ability!)
...Understood. I request leave to cross-examine the witness!
— Impropriety in the Study Tour Selection —
The Ministry of Justice conducted the selection process over a number of stages.
There were written and practical exams, negotiation tests...and finally each candidate was awarded a score.
I was ordered to go undercover and obtained the confidential records.
According to those records, Kazuma Asogi did not place first. He was the runner-up.

The witness's testimony clearly contradicts this evidence!

...that evidence does not appear to have an edge quite as sharp as your glare.

Oh, Runo! I'm counting on you today!

Hm?
